reason: Response B exhibits stylistic hallmarks more characteristic of Claude Opus: extended metaphorical architecture (the liturgical framing sustained across several beats), self-referential philosophical turns ("a ceremony we conduct to honor the work we are conspicuously not doing"), and a preference for layered, subordinate-clause-heavy sentences that build toward an aphoristic kicker. Response A is crisper and more mechanical in its wit, relying on a "conservation law" conceit that feels more like a tidy analogy than the richer, more essayistic voice Opus tends to adopt.
